method.cut_detection: absolute YDIF>20 on per-frame signalstats @30fps, events collapsed within 3 frames
method.why_absolute: median YDIF is 0.06-0.92, so relative thresholds (e.g. 15x median) sit at ~2/255 and fire on noise; verified against scene-detect@0.22 and motion-curve spikes
method.speech: faster-whisper word timings; pause := inter-word gap >= 0.30s
method.caveat: YDIF>20 counts VISUAL DISCONTINUITY. A hard cut between two near-identical talking-head frames is under-counted; a fast pan/zoom inside one take can over-count. Treat +/-10% as noise.

CREATOR_CONSTANTS._note: hold these fixed across every format — this is the person, not the video

speaking_rate_wps.robustness: including the interview moves the mean only 3.76->3.70 and the spread 1.16x->1.17x; the constant holds either way, but it is excluded on principle because it does not measure Nick.

th_edit_cut_rate.note: inset-corrected and conditioned on TALKING-HEAD. This replaces the retracted 10x 'format variable' cut rate.

speech_density.note: tighter than wps. kimi-k3 + fable-tokens = 25 min with NOT ONE SECOND of silence (longest gap 0.98s / 0.94s).

face_never_off_screen.note: zero stretches >=2s where the webcam inset is frozen. The webcam is live in every screen segment.

webcam_inset_area.note: within 1px across videos shot months apart — a rig constant. CORNER and ASPECT are format variables.

card_to_screen_handoff.note: a CARD is never a destination; it names the claim and the screen pays it off

no_third_source.value: n=0 B-roll / stock / memes / chart-inserts / reaction cutaways in 121 min
no_third_source.note: there is a camera and a screen. That is the entire vocabulary.

FORMAT_VARIABLES._note: NEVER use a global mean for these — condition on format or the number is correct for no video

sec_per_cut.\u26d4 RETRACTED: this 10x spread measured browser churn, not editing. See th_edit_cut_rate. Kept only so the error is legible.

screen_churn.\u26d4: NOT AN EDIT PARAMETER. This is how fast the demo changes pages — a property of the subject matter. This is what the retracted 10x 'cut rate' was actually measuring.

screen_share.note: narrow — every solo video is at least half screen

inset_corner.note: per-video choice, held for the whole video. Do not hard-code.

THE_UNIT_OF_ANALYSIS_TRAP.finding: counting SHOTS and counting SECONDS give opposite answers about the same video
THE_UNIT_OF_ANALYSIS_TRAP.implication: targeting 'median shot length' produces a metronomic edit that resembles none of his videos. Target LONG TAKES + PUNCTUATING BURSTS instead.

\u26d4 speaker_metrics_invalid.why: 63% of on-screen time (1753s/2764s) is the GUEST, Justin Lobb — a Maker School member Nick interviews about hitting $20k/mo. The transcript opens in Nick's voice: 'a brief conversation I had with Justin Lobb… a maker school member', answered by 'Hey, Nick. Good to see you again.'
\u26d4 speaker_metrics_invalid.consequence: wps=3.37 for this slug averages TWO speakers and is NOT Nick's delivery rate. Excluded from speaking_rate_wps.
\u26d4 speaker_metrics_invalid.still_valid_for: edit-side metrics (cut placement, shot length, layout grammar) — this IS Nick's channel and Nick's edit, in his interview format.
\u26d4 speaker_metrics_invalid.slug_is_misleading: 'solo-20k' reads as a solo video about earning $20k. It is neither solo nor Nick's earnings. Renaming deferred: the slug is referenced across cached artifacts.

CORRECTIONS.claim: saraev-solo-20k is a long solo talking-head by Nick Saraev
CORRECTIONS.reality: a two-camera remote guest interview; Nick is on screen ~20-37% of it
CORRECTIONS.how_caught: 9-frame montage across the video showed two visibly distinct people in two different rooms; the taxonomy analyst independently segmented it GUEST/NICK/SPLIT and measured 1753s of guest; the transcript names Justin Lobb
CORRECTIONS.why_it_mattered: this single video was the sole outlier on EVERY structural test — the only non-clustered edit (0.6x vs 3.2-3.5x Poisson), the only burst-free video, the only one with cut-to-pause lift below chance. Had it stayed mislabeled as a solo talking head, the pack would have taught that 'his long talking-heads abandon the burst grammar' — a rule derived entirely from a mislabeled file.
CORRECTIONS.lesson: the outlier was not a subtle stylistic truth. It was a filing error wearing one.

CORRECTIONS.claim: cut rate is a FORMAT VARIABLE with a 10x spread (6.8s-68.3s); the burst (~7s, 5-6 shots) is the atomic unit and a creator constant
CORRECTIONS.reality: ~90% of the detected events are browser churn. Real edit rate: 1/62.5s pooled (2.9x spread); conditioned on talking-head 1/25.5s (1.64x) = a CREATOR CONSTANT. ALL 8 'bursts' in fable-websites come from churn; ZERO come from real edit cuts.
CORRECTIONS.how_caught: the segment-taxonomy analyst built an inset-crop detector: a real cut replaces the face box, a tab switch does not. Independently replicated — both methods return 9/94 and 14/113 exactly.
CORRECTIONS.why_it_mattered: the pack would have taught a compositor to cut every 7 seconds in bursts. That is not an edit he has ever made — it is a description of how fast he clicks through a browser. '1 cut per 7.1s' for fable-websites was reporting HOW MANY WEBSITES HE BUILT.
CORRECTIONS.lesson: the measurements were accurate; the INTERPRETATION was invented. Always ask what physically produced the pixels before calling a signal an edit.

CORRECTIONS.claim: he cuts to purpose-built sketch diagrams and slide cards — 'the production value is in the ARTIFACTS, not the EDIT'
CORRECTIONS.reality: the cards are excalidraw.com and FigJam in a browser tab — fable-tokens' is a FigJam doc titled 'Nick Saraev Scratch Sheet' at 135% zoom; fable-websites' is excalidraw at 203% with the tab bar visible. Live whiteboards captured in the same screen recording, not designed assets.
CORRECTIONS.lesson: I 'corrected' a right thesis into a wrong one. The original reading — he adds nothing — was correct.

\u26d4\u26d4 READ FIRST.the_detector_counts_browsing_not_editing: full-frame YDIF>20 detects VISUAL DISCONTINUITY. ~90% of those events in this corpus are NOT edits — they are the browser changing what it shows while the camera keeps rolling. Replicated two ways, agreeing exactly: fable-websites 9 real / 94 raw (10%); sol-ads 14 / 113 (12%).
\u26d4\u26d4 READ FIRST.the_correct_test: every solo video carries a LIVE webcam inset in a fixed box. A real edit cut replaces the whole frame INCLUDING the face; a tab switch leaves the face continuous. real_edit_cut := full-frame YDIF>20 AND inset-crop YDIF>20 within 0.25s.
\u26d4\u26d4 READ FIRST.it_also_misses_cuts: agent-workflow has 26 true cuts vs 17 raw; kimi-k3 has 7 internal jump cuts the full-frame signal scores as 0 (the face is ~6% of frame). Dead air removed behind a static document is invisible to it.
\u26d4\u26d4 READ FIRST.rule: NEVER measure his screen segments with a scene detector. Measure the face.

do_cuts_land_on_speech.status: NOT ESTABLISHED — do not encode a rule in either direction

evidence_at_chance.video: solo-20k interview (every event is a real editorial camera switch, no screen to contaminate)
evidence_at_chance.result: 14.0% land in silence vs 15.7% chance = exactly chance

evidence_weak_association.result: 6/23 within 300ms of a pause, 2.3x lift, p=0.042
evidence_weak_association.weight: weak, marginal, would not survive correction for multiple hypotheses

do_cuts_land_on_speech.not_comparable: the two use different tests (inside-a-gap vs within-300ms-of-a-gap-midpoint)
do_cuts_land_on_speech.\u26d4 the confound: CHURN correlates with pauses — he stops talking briefly to click. That is exactly why the contaminated data showed a confident 2x 'lift'.
